case_name: H.F. v. Board of Trustees, Etc.

summary.brief: H.F. v. Board of Trustees, Etc.. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: H.F. suffered a traumatic event that was caused by an external circumstance
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A physician 'found [H.F.] to be suffering from PTSD.'
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: H.F. was dispatched, along with Sergeants J.V. and A.E., to a report of shots fired.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: H.F. shot the suspect in the chest, and J.V. and A.E. discharged their firearms.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: David listed H.F.'s symptoms and opined he was 'unable to function as a police officer.'
evidence_index.speaker: Medical record

evidence_index.text: David opined within 'a reasonable degree of medical certainty' that H.F. was 'permanently and totally disabled.'
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: We reverse the Board's decision because its heightened interpretation of Richardson factor 2(c) is unsupported.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: We hold Richardson factor 2(c) is satisfied when a member demonstrates he or she has suffered a disabling exacerbation.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: H.F., therefore, is entitled to ADR benefits for the exacerbation of his asymptomatic PTSD.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: Married, father of two, former Marine

initial_state.chief_complaint: Acute stress reaction following a shooting incident
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: H.F. was involved in a shooting incident on May 23, 2020, where he shot a suspect in the chest. Following the incident, he experienced anxiety, nightmares, and flashbacks.

focused_exam.psychiatric: Acute stress reaction observed at the scene

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Acute stress reaction with exacerbation of PTSD

requestables.name: Psychiatric Evaluation
requestables.description: Evaluation by psychiatrist David Gallina, M.D.

reveal.result_summary: H.F. diagnosed with exacerbation of PTSD with dissociative symptoms
reveal.clinical_significance: Confirms exacerbation of PTSD due to shooting incident

timeline_phases.name: Incident and Initial Reaction
timeline_phases.description: H.F. involved in shooting incident and experiences acute stress reaction

timeline_phases.name: Medical Evaluation and Treatment
timeline_phases.description: H.F. undergoes psychiatric evaluation and treatment

decision_points.clinical_context: Evaluation of H.F.'s eligibility for ADR benefits
decision_points.prompt: Should H.F. be granted ADR benefits based on his condition?

options.description: Grant ADR benefits based on exacerbation of PTSD
options.clinical_reasoning: Exacerbation of PTSD due to external traumatic event qualifies for ADR benefits

options.description: Deny ADR benefits due to preexisting condition
options.clinical_reasoning: Misinterpretation of requirement for new onset of mental disease

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Belief that exacerbation of preexisting condition does not qualify

expected_action_court.description: ADR benefits should have been granted
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Exacerbation of PTSD due to traumatic event qualifies

explanation.what_should_have_happened: ADR benefits should have been granted

patient_outcome.description: H.F. diagnosed with exacerbation of PTSD, unable to function as a police officer
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Unable to perform duties as a Sheriff's Officer

key_findings: Board's denial of ADR benefits was incorrect
Exacerbation of PTSD due to traumatic event qualifies for ADR benefits

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: ADR benefits should have been granted

court_findings.finding: Board's denial of ADR benefits was incorrect
